Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> Vandervort produces the "Dori Monson Show" airing weekdays from noon to 3 p.m., PST. "We cover news, politics, current events and pop culture for the Western Washington, Peugeot Sound region," he says. Research studies are okay, particularly when they go "to the heart of human behavior. Scientific, esoteric stuff, is probably not going to get much interest," he says. "KIRO-AM is kind of a heritage station up here in Seattle. We attract the biggest, broadest audience," he says. They tend to appeal to a 25-54 year-old male audience. He'll cover products "like a unique new website. Something that the broadest amount of listeners would be interested in," he says. A new invention like "Puppy Stairs" does not interest him. He wants "guests who are involved in stories first hand, such as lawyers, attorneys and individual politicians. Not someone from a think tank," he says. Contact him via email.
